description: 'raeli' is the vertical extent from a lower reference point to an upper one. The dimension comes before anyone judges the object tall or low. 'raelu' (tall) evaluates that extent, while 'nusho' (depth) measures downward or inward from a reference surface.
articulatory_notes: Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'r' and open into central 'a'. Keep the vowels separate across the syllable boundary, raising the tongue into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: The measured thing commonly appears as a possessor, as in 'shiro raeli' (the tree's height). 'masue' (measure) reports the act. Context supplies the exact unit and reference points.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Practical height measurement begins from an agreed reference point. A body, cord, rod, or marked measure can carry the comparison.

semantic_domains.spatial: distance between lower and upper reference points
